codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements. pause_task() and resume_task() APIs correctly suspend and resume tasks, with proper state preservation during pause via paused_from_state field. The implementation is clean, testable, and follows project conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Transcript skeleton references 'mission-worker-base' skill which does not exist in .factory/skills/. The Rust skill file exists and was used.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript first tool call shows skill='mission-worker-base', but no such skill file exists at .factory/skills/mission-worker-base/SKILL.md

summary: Feature implementation passes review. pause_task() and resume_task() APIs are properly implemented in TaskStateMachine (state_machine.rs:172-200), with the state machine layer correctly dispatching through Orchestrator (lib.rs:488-498). The paused_from_state field correctly tracks previous state for accurate resume restoration. 9 comprehensive state machine tests plus 7 daemon command tests verify valid transitions, invalid transition rejection, and state preservation. No blocking issues found.
